name: chargebacks
title: Chargeback Management
description: API capable of fetching, challenging and accepting chargebacks and managing related disputes

endpoints.description: Submit dispute documents with dispute notes

schema.title: DisputeNotes
schema.description: Notes related to the dispute and associated documents.

disputeNote.description: A note providing additional context or information specifically about the dispute.
disputeNote.example: Customer did not recognize the transaction, initiated dispute.

documentNote.description: A note pertaining to the documents linked to the dispute, summarizing their content or relevance.
documentNote.example: Transaction receipt and customer communication logs attached.

schema.title: SubmitResponse
schema.description: Submit Status

schema.title: Error
schema.description: The error information.

error-details.title: Error Details
error-details.description: The error details. Required for client-side `4XX` errors.

field.description: The field that caused the error. If the field is in the body, set this value to the JSON pointer to that field. Required for client-side errors.

value.description: The value of the field that caused the error.

location.description: The location of the field that caused the error. Value is `body`, `path`, or `query`.

issue.description: The unique fine-grained application-level error code.

description.description: The human-readable description for an issue. The description MAY change over the lifetime of an API, so clients MUST NOT depend on this value.

href.description: The complete target URL, or link, to use in combination with the method to make the related call, as defined by [RFC 6570 - URI Template](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6570), with the addition of the `$`, `(`, and `)` characters for pre-processing. The `href` is the key HATEOAS component that links a completed call with a subsequent call.

rel.description: The [link relation type](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5988#section-4), which is an identifier for a link that unambiguously describes the semantics of the link. For values, see [Link Relationship Types](https://www.iana.org/assignments/link-relations/link-relations.xhtml).

targetMediaType.description: The [RFC 2046-defined media type](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2046.txt) that describes the link target.

targetSchema.description: The schema that describes the link target.

method.description: The method to use to request the link target. For example, for HTTP, this might be `GET` or `DELETE`.

submissionMediaType.description: The media type with which to submit data with the request.

submissionSchema.description: The schema that describes the request data.

name.description: The human-readable, unique name of the error.

correlationId.description: Internal identifier used for correlation purposes.

message.description: The message that describes the error.

informationLink.description: The URI for detailed information related to this error for the developer.

details.description: An array of additional details about the error. Required for client-side `4XX` errors.

items.title: Error Details
items.description: The error details. Required for client-side `4XX` errors.

schema.title: Patch Request
schema.description: An array of JSON patch objects to apply partial updates to resources.

items.title: Patch
items.description: The JSON patch object to apply partial updates to resources.

path.description: The JSON pointer to the target document location at which to complete the operation.

value.description: The value to apply. The `remove` operation does not require a value.

from.description: The JSON pointer to the target document location from which to move the value. Required for the `move` operation.

endpoints.description: Retrieve the dispute information with disputeId

parameters.name: disputeId
parameters.description: Dispute unique identifier in the format of a URN with 'urn:dsp:' prefix followed by a UUID

schema.title: SimpleDispute
schema.description: Dispute details including phase and status information

disputeId.description: Dispute unique identifier in the format of a URN with 'urn:dsp:' prefix followed by a UUID

disputePhaseId.description: Dispute phase unique identifier in the format of a URN with 'urn:dph:' prefix followed by a UUID

chargebackControlNumber.description: External unique identifier for a chargeback

disputePhase.title: Dispute Phase
disputePhase.description: The phase of the dispute. Possible values [First Chargeback, Second Chargeback, Chargeback Reversal, Arbitration, Compliance, Good Faith, Fee, Pre-Arbitration, Pre-Compliance, Pre-Note, Retrieval, Collections, ACH RETURN]

disputePhaseStatus.title: Phase Status
disputePhaseStatus.description: The status of the dispute phase. Possible values [New, Pending Submission, Submitted to Acquirer, Expired, Accepted, Declined, Fee Acknowledged, Fulfilled Merchant, Fulfilled-Acquirer, Reversed, Return Created]

isReviewed.description: Indicate whether merchants have reviewed WIN/LOST status of a chargeback phase

txnAmount.description: The amount associated with the original transaction

chargebackAmount.description: The amount associated with the chargeback

reasonCode.description: The reason Code of the chargeback

isActionable.description: Indicate whether merchants should take an action

isRespondable.description: Indicate whether merchants can respond (upload and submit) to a dispute

isAccepted.description: Indicate whether merchants accepted the chargeback

isRefundSettledIncluded.description: Indicate whether original transaction has settled refunds

items.title: RefundDetail
items.description: Refund details of the original transaction

isSettled.description: Indicate whether the refund transaction is settled

amount.description: The amount associated with the refund transaction

refundDetails.description: A list of refund details of the original transaction

expiresAt.description: A date and time, in [Internet date and time format](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6). Note: The regular expression provides static schematic guidance but does not reject all invalid dates.

processBy.description: A date and time, in [Internet date and time format](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6). Note: The regular expression provides static schematic guidance but does not reject all invalid dates.

receivedAt.description: A date and time, in [Internet date and time format](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6). Note: The regular expression provides static schematic guidance but does not reject all invalid dates.

updatedAt.description: A date and time, in [Internet date and time format](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6). Note: The regular expression provides static schematic guidance but does not reject all invalid dates.

endpoints.description: Retrieve a list of disputes filtered by constraints. This endpoint won't provide refund related details. Please use /disputes/{disputeId} if you need this information

parameters.name: disputeIds
parameters.description: A list of dispute unique identifiers

parameters.name: chargebackControlNumbers
parameters.description: A list of external unique identifiers for chargeback cases

parameters.name: transactionIds
parameters.description: A list of unique identifiers for transactions

parameters.name: businessIds
parameters.description: A list of unique identifiers for businesses

parameters.name: storeId
parameters.description: An unique identifier for storeId

parameters.name: disputePhase
parameters.description: The current phase of the dispute

schema.title: Dispute Phase
schema.description: The phase of the dispute. Possible values [First Chargeback, Second Chargeback, Chargeback Reversal, Arbitration, Compliance, Good Faith, Fee, Pre-Arbitration, Pre-Compliance, Pre-Note, Retrieval, Collections, ACH RETURN]

parameters.name: phaseStatuses
parameters.description: A list of statuses of the dispute phase. Choose to filter by either phaseStatuses or disputeStatus

items.title: Phase Status
items.description: The status of the dispute phase. Possible values [New, Pending Submission, Submitted to Acquirer, Expired, Accepted, Declined, Fee Acknowledged, Fulfilled Merchant, Fulfilled-Acquirer, Reversed, Return Created]

parameters.name: disputeStatus
parameters.description: The overall status of the dispute. Choose to filter by either phaseStatuses or disputeStatus

schema.title: Dispute Status
schema.description: The status of the dispute

parameters.name: isReviewed
parameters.description: Indicate whether merchants have reviewed WIN/LOST status of a chargeback phase

parameters.name: isAccepted
parameters.description: Indicate whether merchants accepted the dispute

parameters.name: isActionable
parameters.description: Indicate whether merchants should take an action.

parameters.name: isRespondable
parameters.description: Indicate whether merchants can upload a document.

parameters.name: reasonCodes
parameters.description: A list of reason Code of the chargeback

parameters.name: processor
parameters.description: The processor of the dispute

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description: The currency in which the amount is specified

schema.title: Currency Code
schema.description: A three-character ISO-4217 currency code.

parameters.name: sortBy
parameters.description: The field by which to sort the results. 'disputeExpiredAt' and 'disputeProcessedBy' share the same enum 'EXPIRED_AT'. Default is 'UPDATED_AT'

parameters.name: sortOrder
parameters.description: The order of sorting, either ascending ('ASC') or descending ('DESC'). Default is descending ('DESC')

parameters.name: processByAfter
parameters.description: Filter disputes to be processed after a given date/time (RFC 3339)

schema.description: A date and time, in [Internet date and time format](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6). Note: The regular expression provides static schematic guidance but does not reject all invalid dates.

parameters.name: processByBefore
parameters.description: Filter disputes to be processed before a given date/time (RFC 3339)

schema.description: A date and time, in [Internet date and time format](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6). Note: The regular expression provides static schematic guidance but does not reject all invalid dates.

parameters.name: expiresAfter
parameters.description: Filter disputes expiring after a specified date/time (RFC 3339)

schema.description: A date and time, in [Internet date and time format](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6). Note: The regular expression provides static schematic guidance but does not reject all invalid dates.

parameters.name: expiresBefore
parameters.description: Filter disputes expiring before a specified date/time (RFC 3339)

schema.description: A date and time, in [Internet date and time format](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6). Note: The regular expression provides static schematic guidance but does not reject all invalid dates.

parameters.name: receivedAfter
parameters.description: Filters for disputes that were received after the specified date and time. The date and time should be RFC 3339 format

schema.description: A date and time, in [Internet date and time format](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6). Note: The regular expression provides static schematic guidance but does not reject all invalid dates.

parameters.name: receivedBefore
parameters.description: Filters for disputes that were received before the specified date and time. The date and time should be RFC 3339 format

schema.description: A date and time, in [Internet date and time format](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6). Note: The regular expression provides static schematic guidance but does not reject all invalid dates.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: The number of records per page. Default is 10

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to retrieve. Default is 1

parameters.name: totalRequired
parameters.description: A boolean that indicates whether the total number of records is required. Default is false

200.description: A list of disputes including pagination

schema.title: SimpleDisputeList
schema.description: Represents a collection of dispute items, each containing key details of individual disputes, along with navigation links for pagination or related actions

items.title: SimpleDispute
items.description: Dispute details including phase and status information

disputeId.description: Dispute unique identifier in the format of a URN with 'urn:dsp:' prefix followed by a UUID

disputePhaseId.description: Dispute phase unique identifier in the format of a URN with 'urn:dph:' prefix followed by a UUID

chargebackControlNumber.description: External unique identifier for a chargeback

disputePhase.title: Dispute Phase
disputePhase.description: The phase of the dispute. Possible values [First Chargeback, Second Chargeback, Chargeback Reversal, Arbitration, Compliance, Good Faith, Fee, Pre-Arbitration, Pre-Compliance, Pre-Note, Retrieval, Collections, ACH RETURN]

disputePhaseStatus.title: Phase Status
disputePhaseStatus.description: The status of the dispute phase. Possible values [New, Pending Submission, Submitted to Acquirer, Expired, Accepted, Declined, Fee Acknowledged, Fulfilled Merchant, Fulfilled-Acquirer, Reversed, Return Created]

isReviewed.description: Indicate whether merchants have reviewed WIN/LOST status of a chargeback phase

txnAmount.description: The amount associated with the original transaction

chargebackAmount.description: The amount associated with the chargeback

reasonCode.description: The reason Code of the chargeback

isActionable.description: Indicate whether merchants should take an action

isRespondable.description: Indicate whether merchants can respond (upload and submit) to a dispute

isAccepted.description: Indicate whether merchants accepted the chargeback

isRefundSettledIncluded.description: Indicate whether original transaction has settled refunds

items.title: RefundDetail
items.description: Refund details of the original transaction

isSettled.description: Indicate whether the refund transaction is settled

amount.description: The amount associated with the refund transaction

refundDetails.description: A list of refund details of the original transaction

expiresAt.description: A date and time, in [Internet date and time format](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6). Note: The regular expression provides static schematic guidance but does not reject all invalid dates.

processBy.description: A date and time, in [Internet date and time format](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6). Note: The regular expression provides static schematic guidance but does not reject all invalid dates.

receivedAt.description: A date and time, in [Internet date and time format](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6). Note: The regular expression provides static schematic guidance but does not reject all invalid dates.

updatedAt.description: A date and time, in [Internet date and time format](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6). Note: The regular expression provides static schematic guidance but does not reject all invalid dates.

items.description: An array of dispute items

totalItems.description: Indicate the total number of items in the full result list. Only be returned when totalRequired is set to true

totalPages.description: Indicate the total number of pages of response available. Only be returned when totalRequired is set to true

links.description: An array of link objects providing navigational URLs

endpoints.summary: Retrieve a list of dispute documents
endpoints.description: Retrieve a list of dispute documents

parameters.name: disputeId
parameters.description: Dispute unique identifier in the format of a URN with 'urn:dsp:' prefix followed by a UUID

parameters.name: disputeDocumentIds
parameters.description: A list of dispute document unique identifiers

parameters.name: disputePhase
parameters.description: The current phase of the dispute

schema.title: Phase Status
schema.description: The status of the dispute phase. Possible values [New, Pending Submission, Submitted to Acquirer, Expired, Accepted, Declined, Fee Acknowledged, Fulfilled Merchant, Fulfilled-Acquirer, Reversed, Return Created]

parameters.name: deleted
parameters.description: Indicate whether the document is deleted

parameters.name: startTime
parameters.description: Filters for dispute documents that were created after the specified date and time. The date and time should be in RFC 3339 format

schema.description: A date and time, in [Internet date and time format](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6). Note: The regular expression provides static schematic guidance but does not reject all invalid dates.

parameters.name: endTime
parameters.description: Filters for dispute documents that were created before the specified date and time. The date and time should be in RFC 3339 format

schema.description: A date and time, in [Internet date and time format](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6). Note: The regular expression provides static schematic guidance but does not reject all invalid dates.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: The number of records per page. Default is 10

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to retrieve. Default is 1

parameters.name: totalRequired
parameters.description: A boolean that indicates whether the total number of records is required. Default is false

schema.title: DisputeDocumentList
schema.description: Represents a collection of dispute document items, each containing key details of individual dispute documents, along with navigation links for pagination or related actions

items.title: SimpleDisputeDocument
items.description: Dispute document details

disputeDocumentId.description: Dispute Document unique identifier in the format of a URN with 'urn:doc:' prefix followed by a UUID

disputeId.description: Dispute unique identifier in the format of a URN with 'urn:dsp:' prefix followed by a UUID

filename.description: The name of the dispute document file

documentType.description: Type of the document. Possible values include [Drivers License, Tax Return, Passport, Trade, KYC, Other]

mimeType.description: MIME type of the document. Possible values include [application/pdf, image/tiff, image/jpeg]

disputePhase.title: Dispute Phase
disputePhase.description: The phase of the dispute. Possible values [First Chargeback, Second Chargeback, Chargeback Reversal, Arbitration, Compliance, Good Faith, Fee, Pre-Arbitration, Pre-Compliance, Pre-Note, Retrieval, Collections, ACH RETURN]

status.description: Document upload and submit status. Possible value include [Inprogress, SUCCESS, Successfully Uploaded, DOC_SZ_LRG, Scan Failed]. The final successful stage is 'Successfully Uploaded'

href.description: The complete target URL, or link, to use in combination with the method to make the related call, as defined by [RFC 6570 - URI Template](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6570), with the addition of the `$`, `(`, and `)` characters for pre-processing. The `href` is the key HATEOAS component that links a completed call with a subsequent call.

rel.description: The [link relation type](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5988#section-4), which is an identifier for a link that unambiguously describes the semantics of the link. For values, see [Link Relationship Types](https://www.iana.org/assignments/link-relations/link-relations.xhtml).

targetMediaType.description: The [RFC 2046-defined media type](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2046.txt) that describes the link target.

targetSchema.description: The schema that describes the link target.

method.description: The method to use to request the link target. For example, for HTTP, this might be `GET` or `DELETE`.

submissionMediaType.description: The media type with which to submit data with the request.

submissionSchema.description: The schema that describes the request data.

submittedAt.description: A date and time, in [Internet date and time format](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6). Note: The regular expression provides static schematic guidance but does not reject all invalid dates.

createdAt.description: A date and time, in [Internet date and time format](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6). Note: The regular expression provides static schematic guidance but does not reject all invalid dates.

updatedAt.description: A date and time, in [Internet date and time format](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6). Note: The regular expression provides static schematic guidance but does not reject all invalid dates.

items.description: An array of dispute document items

totalItems.description: Indicate the total number of items in the full result list. Only be returned when totalRequired is set to true

totalPages.description: Indicate the total number of pages of response available. Only be returned when totalRequired is set to true

links.description: An array of link objects providing navigational URLs

links.description: An array of error-related HATEOAS links.

items.title: Link Description
items.description: A request-related [HATEOAS link](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-handrews-json-schema-hyperschema-02).

href.description: The complete target URL, or link, to use in combination with the method to make the related call, as defined by [RFC 6570 - URI Template](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6570), with the addition of the `$`, `(`, and `)` characters for pre-processing. The `href` is the key HATEOAS component that links a completed call with a subsequent call.

rel.description: The [link relation type](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5988#section-4), which is an identifier for a link that unambiguously describes the semantics of the link. For values, see [Link Relationship Types](https://www.iana.org/assignments/link-relations/link-relations.xhtml).

targetMediaType.description: The [RFC 2046-defined media type](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2046.txt) that describes the link target.

targetSchema.description: The schema that describes the link target.

method.description: The method to use to request the link target. For example, for HTTP, this might be `GET` or `DELETE`.

submissionMediaType.description: The media type with which to submit data with the request.

submissionSchema.description: The schema that describes the request data.
